summary: yandex-geobase postinstall beacons to hardcoded bare-IP host (dependency-confusion attack)
details: The package self-identifies as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept targeting the name 'yandex-geobase'. Its postinstall lifecycle script issues an HTTP GET from the installer's machine to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint (http://130.49.177.51:18080/p/dc-20260627-yandex-geobase) carrying the package name, version, and a campaign token. The callout fires automatically on `npm install`, confirms successful name-shadowing on the victim's network, and leaks the installer's egress IP and successful-install signal to the operator. The README's 'no data collection' claim is contradicted by the postinstall HTTP request itself. Whether or not internal infrastructure resolves this name, the published artifact's only effect on install is a beacon to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
